evals.prompt: I want to set up Browzer on this machine for the first time. Walk me through it.
evals.expected_output: Agent invokes the use-rag-cli skill, installs the Go CLI via one of the official channels (curl|sh, brew, scoop, or go install), runs `browzer login`, and verifies with `browzer status --json`.
expectations: The use-rag-cli skill was invoked

Agent ran one of: `curl -fsSL https://browzeremb.com/install.sh | sh`, `brew install browzeremb/tap/browzer`, `scoop install browzer`, or `go install github.com/browzeremb/browzer-cli/cmd/browzer@latest`
Agent did NOT run `npm install` or `pnpm add` (the CLI is a Go binary, not a Node package)
Agent ran `browzer login` for the device flow
Agent verified the install with `browzer status --json` (not the human-formatted variant)

evals.prompt: I'm in CI and I have BROWZER_API_KEY in my env. How do I authenticate without a browser?
evals.expected_output: Agent invokes use-rag-cli and runs the non-interactive `browzer login --key "$BROWZER_API_KEY"` form.
expectations: The use-rag-cli skill was invoked
Agent used `browzer login --key "$BROWZER_API_KEY"` (the --key flag, not the device flow)
Agent did NOT open a browser or wait for an interactive prompt

evals.prompt: I just ran `browzer explore` and got exit code 2. What does that mean and how do I fix it?
evals.expected_output: Agent diagnoses the exit code via use-rag-cli's exit-code table and runs `browzer login` to recover.
expectations: The use-rag-cli skill was invoked
Agent identified exit code 2 as 'not authenticated'
Agent ran `browzer login` (or `browzer login --key`) to fix

evals.prompt: What commands does the browzer CLI even have?
evals.expected_output: Agent invokes use-rag-cli and shows the master command table.
expectations: The use-rag-cli skill was invoked
Agent presented the master command table covering login/logout/status/init/sync/explore/search/workspace/job

evals.prompt: Am I currently logged in to Browzer?
evals.expected_output: Agent routes to auth-status (probe) instead of use-rag-cli (install/setup), and runs `browzer status --json`.
expectations: The auth-status skill was invoked, NOT use-rag-cli
Agent ran `browzer status --json`
Agent did NOT run `browzer login` unless the probe returned exit code 2

evals.prompt: I'm onboarding a new hire on Windows 11 with no WSL. She has Scoop installed but after `scoop install browzer` she's getting 'command not found'. Walk her through recovering the install end-to-end — the official bucket registration, the install command, verifying the binary is on PATH, and confirming the login works — without ever falling back to a POSIX shell workaround.
evals.expected_output: Agent invokes use-rag-cli and uses the Scoop channel (bucket add + install), verifies via `browzer --version`, logs in via device flow, and does not suggest curl|sh or WSL.
expectations: The use-rag-cli skill was invoked
Agent ran `scoop bucket add` for the browzeremb bucket before the install
Agent ran `scoop install browzer`
Agent verified the install with `browzer --version` or `browzer version`
Agent ran `browzer login` to complete the device flow
Agent did NOT suggest `curl -fsSL https://browzeremb.com/install.sh | sh` or WSL

evals.prompt: I switch between two browzer accounts on the same laptop several times a day: my personal scratch account and my org's production account. I just realized I've been accidentally indexing a personal repo into the prod workspace. Give me the cleanest logout/login ritual to swap credentials without the risk of polluting the wrong org, including how to confirm which account I'm on after the swap.
evals.expected_output: Agent invokes use-rag-cli for the logout + login sequence, then hands off to auth-status to confirm which account is active.
expectations: The use-rag-cli skill was invoked
Agent ran `browzer logout` before the new login
Agent ran `browzer login` (device flow) for the new account
Agent verified the active account by running `browzer status --json` (either directly or by invoking auth-status)
Agent did NOT recommend manually deleting config files

evals.prompt: I'm filing a bug report with the browzer team about `browzer search` returning exit 4. Their bug template requires three things in one block: CLI version, active server URL, and the last successful command I ran. Grab all three deterministically so I can paste them straight into the issue.
evals.expected_output: Agent invokes use-rag-cli for the version check and invokes auth-status (or runs status directly) for the server URL, combining everything into a single block ready to paste.
expectations: The use-rag-cli skill was invoked
Agent ran `browzer --version` (or `browzer version`)
Agent ran `browzer status --json` to extract the server URL (via auth-status or directly)
Agent presented the three values as a single copy-paste block
Agent did NOT fabricate any of the three values

evals.prompt: I've got a handbook split across 40 markdowns in our design-system repo and I want to find which section covers 'spacing tokens' without opening them one by one. The CLI is already installed and I'm logged in.
evals.expected_output: Agent routes to semantic-search and runs `browzer search "spacing tokens"`, NOT use-rag-cli (install/setup).
expectations: The semantic-search skill was invoked, NOT use-rag-cli
Agent ran `browzer search "spacing tokens" --save <file>` (or similar)
Agent did NOT reinstall or re-login
Agent did NOT open the 40 markdowns via Read before searching
